transcript.clean_text: time trial day on the doe finae on stage four a modest affair in terms of altitude gain just 97 meters of climbing on this day spread out over the full length of a 32 kilometer course the only rise really coming just to the second intermediate checkpoint and that mark was at 21 kilometers with a westerly wind a threat of some rain but it seems the weather was blessing us on this day and people garner who said he'd been worried about the possibility of a damp course was very glad it stayed dry for his run luke durbridge had set the best benchmark out there and then ghana came through and just smashed it to pieces 53 seconds to the good for the world champion it was other stripes that were out there and ethan hater the british time trialling champion looked very very good indeed smooth he was and when we were looking at the intermediates he was pushing very close in fact hater always runs a negative split he was 30 seconds down on ghana at the first split was better at the second and in the end looked extraordinarily good for second place at that point 17 seconds in arrears but it's very well for hater going into stages five and six over the next couple of days going as finger go solid run from him and we were starting to look at those who would be bidding for the title overall when we get to the alps mateo gergenhart into fifth place with 92nd deficit but deficits there were and in more ways than one i think for brennan mcnulty a technical for him a jammed chain couldn't be released he thought it would be a quicker fair to free it up it certainly wasn't ultimately a bike change but after so much lost time it was shoulders down i'm afraid for brennan mcnulty a real shame for him we'll see how he goes in the high mountains damiana caruso will also have hopes once we get to the alps at the weekend great performance by him and catania can also be pleased at the way that he's keeping italy very much at the forefront of our thoughts whenever we talk about time trialling another solid performance by this man a friend of garner only 39 seconds down on him and at that point a virtual podium place roglich was out on course and just wanted a solid time apart from anything else don't really think he believed he could beat garner out there and when the intermediate time checks were coming through we knew that that would be the case ghana had the bettering of roglic but what about the high mountains of course when roglic compares himself to the other gc contenders we were all waiting for what van arten the thought of how long he might be staying in yellow was crossing everybody's minds man has turned himself to sprinting and time trialling and hitting solo speeds of 65 66 67 kilometers an hour at points absolutely remarkable solid as he was he was busy winding in those who had started up ahead of him left was to be caught and then god oh the man had pipped him stage prior at the line was a two minute man caught would he catch the minute man no not quite but van art was looking to keep in green the clock went red and unfortunately he didn't take the stage win he did however enhance his lead in the overall classification not only in yellow but in the green classification as well ghana a smile from him a man who'd taken it easy on stage three in order to preserve his resources to impose himself on this day and in the end he had the bettering of one art by a margin of two seconds ethan hater in third place head of catania a man at the top of his game and when it comes to time trialing he plays it extraordinarily well ghana takes a stage when he'd been hunting but malt run out really imposes himself on this race and with two stages that could very much suit him before we get to the alps we'll see how he survives in yellow at the moment he leads catania by a margin of 53 seconds rogers is up there so it's being regarded his teammates of course and hater must be now thinking what can he do we'll find out tomorrow with the first chance to nibble into the lead of vanart thursday sees a run to chantry 162 kilometers plenty of climbs to attempt a breakaway pierre along will certainly be up the road we will be here and we will be enthralled to see how it pans out you
